data.title: Under Armour plans to separate Curry Brand as part of its expanded restructuring plan
data.description:
Under Armour (UAA) said on Thursday that the company and basketball star Stephen Curry have planned to separate Curry Brand from Under Armour. 

Under Armour will develop new UA Basketball products and continue to support athletes and programs across every level of the game, the company said. 

Under the separation, Curry will become independent of Under Armour. UA will release the Curry 13—the final Curry Brand x Under Armour shoe—in February 2026 as planned, with additional colorways and apparel collections available through October 2026.

Under Armour (UAA) also announced the expansion of its previously disclosed fiscal 2025 restructuring plan and increased its fiscal 2026 adjusted operating income outlook.

Previously, the company anticipated incurring up to $160M in pre-tax restructuring and related charges in connection with its fiscal 2025 restructuring plan. Following further review, Under Armour's board has approved an additional $95M in restructuring actions, the primary benefits of which will be realized in future periods. 

This includes the separation of the Curry Brand from Under Armour, further contract terminations, incremental asset impairments, and additional employee severance and benefits costs.

The company estimates that its total global basketball business, including Curry Brand, will approximate $100M to $120M in revenue for fiscal 2026. In connection with the separation of the Curry Brand, the company does not anticipate a significant effect on its consolidated financial results or profitability.

The expansion of the restructuring and transformation plan brings the total estimated restructuring and related charges to up to $255M. 

As of September 30, 2025, Under Armour had incurred approximately $147M of restructuring and related charges. 

Under Armour also raised its fiscal 2026 adjusted operating income outlook. 

On a GAAP-basis, the company now expects an operating loss of $56M to $71M versus its previous expectation of operating income of $19M to $34M. Adjusted operating income is now expected to reach $95M to $110M, compared to the prior range of $90M to $105M.

data.title: Seth Klarman's Baupost takes stake in Union Pacific, exits ViaSat, among Q3 trades
data.description:
Baupost acquired new stakes in Union Pacific (UNP), boosted holdings in Restaurant Brands (QSR), and reduced its holdings in Alphabet C shares (GOOG), according to its latest 13F filing on Thursday.

The hedge fund took new stakes in Union Pacific (UNP) with ~1.50M shares valued at $353.7M, Genuine Parts (GPC) with ~1.40M shares valued at $193.3M, and Americold (COLD) with 3.6M shares valued at $44.1M.

Baupost increased stakes in Restaurant Brands International (QSR) to 8.25M shares (value $529.3M) from 4.05M, Elevance Health (ELV) to 1.32M shares (value $426.2M) from 616K, and Eagle Materials (EXP) to 939.7K shares (value $219.0M) from 676.3K.

The firm exited Icon Plc (ICLR) and ViaSat (VSAT). It reduced its takes in Alphabet (GOOG) to 1.86M shares (value $452.6M) from 2.63M, Wesco International (WCC) to 1.50M shares (value $317.9M) from ~2.21M shares and Liberty Global class C shares (LBTYK) to 21.7M shares (value $254.5M) from ~24.7M shares, according to the filing.

data.title: Ecopetrol reports Q3 results
data.description:

data.title: ECN Capital to be acquired by Warburg Pincus-led investor group for C$3.10 per share
data.description:
• ECN Capital (ECN.TO) said on Wednesday it has entered into a definitive agreement to be acquired by a Warburg Pincus-led investor group in an all-cash transaction.
  • Under the deal, ECN’s common shareholders will receive C$3.10 per share, representing a roughly 13% premium to the stock’s November 12 closing price of C$2.75. The agreement values the company at about C$1.9 billion.
  • The transaction, unanimously approved by ECN’s board and a special committee of independent directors, also includes cash payments for the company’s Series C and Series E preferred shares.
  • The deal, subject to shareholder and court approval and customary closing conditions, is expected to close in the first half of 2026.
  • Following closing, ECN’s common shares will be delisted from the Toronto Stock Exchange, while Series C shares will be delisted only if shareholders approve the transaction.
  • ECN:CA closed +11.63% at $3.07.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Figure Technology confidentially submits draft statement for public offering of tokenized stock
data.description:
• Figure Technology Solutions (FIGR) said on Thursday that it has confidentially submitted a draft registration statement with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission relating to the proposed public offering of its Series A Blockchain Common Stock, par value $0.0001 per share. 
  • The Tokenized Stock will not be listed on any legacy exchange but will trade on Figure’s alternative trading system and will be convertible into shares of Class A Common Stock on a one-for-one basis.
  • The offering will be 100% synthetic secondary and therefore non-dilutive to existing shareholders. 
  • The selling shareholders will sell shares of Class A Common Stock that will be exchanged for shares of Tokenized Stock, which will be delivered to wallets on the Provenance blockchain. 
  • The number of shares of Tokenized Stock to be offered and the price range for the proposed offering have not yet been determined. 
  • Figure Technology Solutions (FIGR) shares were up more than 4% in extended trading.
